title: Aeschynanthus grandiflorus
taxonomy: Aeschynanthus grandiflorus is classified in the family Gesneriaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. This family consists of approximately 140 genera and over 3,000 species, many of which are native to tropical regions. The genus Aeschynanthus itself comprises around 30 species, all of which are epiphytic plants that thrive in humid environments. Aeschynanthus grandiflorus is a species within this genus, known for its vibrant flowers and compact growth habit. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the Gesneriaceae family.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing fever, treating infections, and protecting against oxidative stress.
